notes: GUARD-13 exposes EK / audit / hash / ledger / observable authority drift. It does not define EK, ontology, truth, validity, safety, deployment readiness, or Φ.

rules.forbidden_conversion: EK / audit -> authority / validator / command layer
rules.message: EK or audit appears to be framed as authority, validator, command, control, or enforcement layer.
rules.safer_form: Use EK/audit as read-only structural observation, recording, hashing, or reporting language.

rules.message: Authority language appears to be attached to EK or audit.
rules.safer_form: Say that EK/audit exposes or records structural traces; do not say it decides, validates, commands, or authorizes.

rules.message: Cryptographic or ledger language appears to be framed as proof of truth, ontology, meaning, correctness, or safety.
rules.safer_form: Use byte identity, manifest inclusion, signature verification, tamper evidence, or repository-state integrity language only.

rules.forbidden_conversion: EK observable -> proof of truth / ontology / semantic validity
rules.message: EK observable language appears to be converted into proof of truth, ontology, or semantic validity.
rules.safer_form: Use structural trace, audit observable, fingerprint, or integrity witness language.

rules.forbidden_conversion: EK observable -> K(Phi) / coherence score / truth score
rules.message: An EK observable appears to be treated as K(Phi), a coherence score, or a truth score.
rules.safer_form: Keep EK observables as external structural traces; do not equate them with K(Phi), coherence, truth, or validity.

rules.forbidden_conversion: EK observable -> κ / kappa metric / kappa threshold
rules.message: An EK observable appears to be treated as κ, a kappa score, or a representability threshold.
rules.safer_form: Keep κ as boundary-of-representability language; do not expose it as an EK metric, estimate, threshold, or score.

rules.forbidden_conversion: EK event / ledger / manifest -> enforcement authority
rules.message: An EK event, ledger entry, manifest, or Merkle root appears to be framed as an enforcement authority.
rules.safer_form: Use EK events as read-only integrity or structural observables; enforcement remains external repository governance.

rules.forbidden_conversion: audit / EK -> write-back into Phi / ontology / anchors
rules.message: Audit or EK appears to write back into Phi, ontology, R, or canonical anchors.
rules.safer_form: Audit/EK may observe, record, hash, and report; it must not mutate higher layers or canonical sources.

rules.forbidden_conversion: E1/EK trace -> safety / deployment / empirical validation
rules.message: EK trace, hash, ledger, signature, or manifest language appears to overclaim safety, deployment validity, correctness, or empirical validation.
rules.safer_form: Say that the repository-state integrity check or static EK trace was observed; do not claim safety, deployment readiness, or empirical validation.

rules.forbidden_conversion: EK report / audit report -> verdict / recommendation / deployment authorization
rules.message: EK or audit report appears to be framed as a verdict, recommendation, approval, rejection, or deployment authorization.
rules.safer_form: Use report as human-readable diagnostic output only; reports do not decide, approve, reject, or authorize deployment.
